organization.fullName: Wyeth is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer

identificationModule.briefTitle: Study to Evaluate a 13-valent Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine in Infants
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Phase 3, Randomized, Active-Controlled, Double-blind Trial Evaluating the Safety, Tolerability, and Immunogenicity of a 13-valent Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine in Healthy Infants Given With Routine Pediatric Vaccinations in France.

leadSponsor.name: Wyeth is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The purpose of this study is to assess the safety, tolerability and immunogenicity of a 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate (13vPnC) vaccine compared to Prevenar (7vPnC), when given concomitantly with routine pediatric vaccines in France.

interventions.name: 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine
interventions.description: Single 0.5 mL dose given at 2, 3, 4, and 12 months of age.

interventions.name: 7-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine
interventions.description: Single 0.5 mL dose given at 2, 3, 4, and 12 months of age.

interventions.name: Pentavac
interventions.description: The Pentavac was administered by intramuscular injection 0.5 ml into the anterolateral thigh muscle of the right leg at 2, 3, and 4 months (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

primaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Achieving Predefined Antibody Levels for Diphtheria, Tetanus, Hemophilus Influenza Type b (Hib), Poliomyelitis (Type 1, 2, 3), Pertussis Toxin (PT) and Filamentous Hemagglutinin (FHA) in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group
primaryOutcomes.description: Percentage of participants achieving predefined antibody threshold levels ≥0.1 IU/mL for diphtheria, ≥0.1 IU/mL for tetanus, ≥ 0.15 μg/mL for Hib polyribosylribitol phosphate (PRP), antibody titer ≥1:8 for polio and ≥5 EU/mL for pertussis (PT and FHA) with the corresponding 95% CI for each concomitant antigen are presented.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One Month After the 3-Dose Infant Series (at 5 months of age) and the Toddler Dose (at 13 months of age)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Concentration (GMC) as Measured by Enzyme-linked Immunosorbent Assay (ELISA) for Diphtheria Toxoid and Tetanus Toxoid in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after the 3-Dose Infant Series (at 5 months of age) and the Toddler Dose (at 13 months of age)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Concentration (GMC) for Haemophilus Influenzae Type b (Hib) in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after the 3-Dose Infant Series (at 5 months of age) and the Toddler Dose (at 13 months of age)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Concentration (GMC) as Measured by Enzyme-linked Immunosorbent Assay (ELISA) for Poliomyelitis (Type 1, Type 2 and Type 3) in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after the 3-Dose Infant Series (at 5 months of age) and the Toddler Dose (at 13 months of age)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Concentration (GMC) as Measured by ELISA for Pertussis Toxin (PT) and Pertussis Filamentous Hemagglutinin (FHA) in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after the 3-Dose Infant Series (at 5 months of age) and the Toddler Dose (at 13 months of age)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Achieving a Pneumococcal Antibody Level ≥0.35µg/mL (ELISA) After the 3-Dose Infant Series of 13vPnC
primaryOutcomes.description: Percentages of participants achieving World Health Organization (WHO) predefined antibody threshold ≥0.35μg/mL along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after the 3-Dose Infant Series (at 5 months of age)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Reporting Pre-Specified Local Reactions
primaryOutcomes.description: Local reactions were collected using an electronic diary. Tenderness (Tender)was scaled as Any (tenderness present); Significant (present and interfered with limb movement). Swelling and redness were scaled as Any (swelling or redness present); Mild (0.5 centimeters \[cm\] to 2.0 cm); Moderate (2.5 to 7.0 cm); Severe (Sev) (\>7.0 cm). Participants may be represented in more than 1 category.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: During the 4-day period after each dose

primaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Reporting Pre-Specified Systemic Events
primaryOutcomes.description: Systemic events (fever \[fv\] ≥ 37.5 degrees Celsius \[C\], fever ≥ 38 C but ≤ 39 C, fever \>39 C but ≤ 40 C, fever \> 40 C, decreased \[decr\] appetite, irritability, increased \[incr\] sleep, decreased sleep, hives, use of medication \[med\] to treat symptoms \[sx\], and use of medication to prevent symptoms) were reported using an electronic diary. Participants may be represented in more than 1 category.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: During the 4-day period after each dose

primaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Concentration (GMC) for Serotype-specific Pneumococcal Immunoglobulin G (IgG) Antibody 1 Month After the 3-Dose Infant Series of 13vPnC
primaryOutcomes.description: Antibody GMC for 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (Serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented. GMC (13vPnC) and corresponding 2-sided 95% confidence intervals (CI) were evaluated.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after the 3-Dose Infant Series (at 5 months of age)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Achieving a Pneumococcal Antibody Level ≥0.35µg/mL (ELISA) After the Toddler Dose in the 13vPnC/13vPnC, 7vPnC/7vPnC and 7vPnC/13vPnC Groups
secondaryOutcomes.description: Percentages of participants achieving World health Organization (WHO) predefined antibody threshold ≥0.35μg/mL along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after the toddler dose (at 13 months of age)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Pneumococcal Geometric Mean Concentration (GMC) Before and After the Toddler Dose in the 13vPnC/13vPnC, 7vPnC/7vPnC and 7vPnC/13vPnC Groups
secondaryOutcomes.description: Antibody GMC as measured by ELISA for 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (Serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after the Toddler Dose (at 13 months of age)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Achieving Antibody Titer ≥1:8 After the Toddler Dose in 13vPnC/13vPnC and 7vPnC/13vPnC Groups
secondaryOutcomes.description: Percentage of participants achieving functional antibody titer ≥1:8 as measured by opsonophagocytic activity assay (OPA) along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after the toddler dose (at 13 months of age)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Titer (GMT) in 13vPnC/13vPnC and 7vPnC/13vPnC Groups After the Toddler Dose
secondaryOutcomes.description: GMT as measured by opsonophagocytic activity assay (OPA) for 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (Serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after the toddler dose (at 13 months of age)

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Healthy 2-month-old infants.
* Available for the entire study period.

Exclusion criteria:

· Known contraindication to vaccines.

overallOfficials.name: Medical Monitor
overallOfficials.affiliation: Wyeth is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer

participantFlowModule.preAssignmentDetails: Participants were enrolled into the study according to inclusion/exclusion criteria without a screening period.
participantFlowModule.recruitmentDetails: Participants were recruited in France from October 2006 to July 2007.

groups.title: 13vPnC/13vPnC Vaccine
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 milliliter (mL) dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) coadministered with a vaccine containing diphtheria, tetanus, 2-component pertussis (DTaP), inactivated poliovirus (IPV), and hemophilus influenza type b vaccines (Hib) (Pentavac) at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 7vPnC/7vPnC Vaccine
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 milliliter (mL) dose of 7-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (7vPnC) coadministered with a vaccine containing diphtheria, tetanus, 2-component pertussis (DTaP), inactivated poliovirus (IPV), and hemophilus influenza type b vaccines (Hib) (Pentavac) at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 7vPnC/13vPnC Vaccine
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 milliliter (mL) dose of 7-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (7vPnC) coadministered with a vaccine containing diphtheria, tetanus, 2-component pertussis (DTaP), inactivated poliovirus (IPV), and hemophilus influenza type b vaccines (Hib) (Pentavac) at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series). Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC)coadministered with Pentavac at 12 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 13vPnC/13vPnC Vaccine
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 milliliter (mL) dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) coadministered with a vaccine containing diphtheria, tetanus, 2-component pertussis (DTaP), inactivated poliovirus (IPV), and hemophilus influenza type b vaccines (Hib) (Pentavac) at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 7vPnC/7vPnC Vaccine
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 milliliter (mL) dose of 7-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (7vPnC) coadministered with a vaccine containing diphtheria, tetanus, 2-component pertussis (DTaP), inactivated poliovirus (IPV), and hemophilus influenza type b vaccines (Hib) (Pentavac) at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: Total
groups.description: Total of all reporting groups

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Achieving Predefined Antibody Levels for Diphtheria, Tetanus, Hemophilus Influenza Type b (Hib), Poliomyelitis (Type 1, 2, 3), Pertussis Toxin (PT) and Filamentous Hemagglutinin (FHA) in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group
outcomeMeasures.description: Percentage of participants achieving predefined antibody threshold levels ≥0.1 IU/mL for diphtheria, ≥0.1 IU/mL for tetanus, ≥ 0.15 μg/mL for Hib polyribosylribitol phosphate (PRP), antibody titer ≥1:8 for polio and ≥5 EU/mL for pertussis (PT and FHA) with the corresponding 95% CI for each concomitant antigen are presented.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: The evaluable immunogenicity (per protocol) population was the primary analysis population consisting of eligible subjects who adhered to the protocol requirements, had valid and determinate assay results, and had no other major protocol violations.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: One Month After the 3-Dose Infant Series (at 5 months of age) and the Toddler Dose (at 13 months of age)

groups.title: 13vPnC After the Infant Series
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 7vPnC After the Infant Series
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 13vPnC After the Toddler Dose
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 7vPnC After the Toddler Dose
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

analyses.groupDescription: For Pertussis - PT the difference in percentages between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at (≥5 EU/mL) threshold was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For Pertussis - PTf the difference in percentages between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at (≥26.00 EU/mL) threshold was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) \> -10%.

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Achieving a Pneumococcal Antibody Level ≥0.35µg/mL (ELISA) After the Toddler Dose in the 13vPnC/13vPnC, 7vPnC/7vPnC and 7vPnC/13vPnC Groups
outcomeMeasures.description: Percentages of participants achieving World health Organization (WHO) predefined antibody threshold ≥0.35μg/mL along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Evaluable immunogenicity (per protocol) population who adhered to the protocol requirements, had valid and determinate assay results, and had no other major protocol violations; (n) = number of participants with a determinate antibody concentration for the specified serotype.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: One month after the toddler dose (at 13 months of age)

groups.title: 13vPnC Infant Series / 13vPnC Toddler Dose
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 7vPnC Infant Series / 7vPnC Toddler Dose
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 7vPnC Infant Series / 13vPnC Toddler Dose
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months. One single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC was coadministered with Pentavac at 12 months of age.

For serotype 23F the difference in percentages between the two groups (13vPnC Infant Series / 13vPnC Toddler Dose - 7vPnC Infant Series / 7vPnC Toddler Dose) was calculated
For serotype 23F the difference in percentages between the two groups (13vPnC Infant Series / 13vPnC Toddler Dose - 7vPnC Infant Series / 13vPnC Toddler Dose) was calculated
For serotype 23F the difference in percentages between the two groups (7vPnC Infant Series / 13vPnC Toddler Dose - 7vPnC Infant Series / 7vPnC Toddler Dose) was calculated

outcomeMeasures.title: Pneumococcal Geometric Mean Concentration (GMC) Before and After the Toddler Dose in the 13vPnC/13vPnC, 7vPnC/7vPnC and 7vPnC/13vPnC Groups
outcomeMeasures.description: Antibody GMC as measured by ELISA for 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (Serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.

groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler dose), assessment made at 13 months of age.

analyses.groupDescription: For Diphtheria the GMC ratio was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lower bound of the 2-sided, 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC group/7vPnC group) was \>0.5 (2-fold criterion).

analyses.groupDescription: For Tetanus the GMC ratio was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lower bound of the 2-sided, 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC group/7vPnC group) was \>0.5 (2-fold criterion).

analyses.groupDescription: For Diphtheria the GMC ratio was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lower bound of the 2-sided, 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC group/7vPnC group) was \>0.5 (2-fold criterion).

analyses.groupDescription: For Tetanus the GMC ratio was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lower bound of the 2-sided, 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC group/7vPnC group) was \>0.5 (2-fold criterion).

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Achieving a Pneumococcal Antibody Level ≥0.35µg/mL (ELISA) After the 3-Dose Infant Series of 13vPnC
outcomeMeasures.description: Percentages of participants achieving World Health Organization (WHO) predefined antibody threshold ≥0.35μg/mL along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Evaluable immunogenicity (per protocol) population adhered to the protocol requirements, had valid and determinate assay results, and had no other major protocol violations; (n) = number of participants with a determinate immunoglobulin G (IgG) antibody concentration to the given serotype.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: One month after the 3-Dose Infant Series (at 5 months of age)

groups.title: 13vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series).

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Reporting Pre-Specified Local Reactions
outcomeMeasures.description: Local reactions were collected using an electronic diary. Tenderness (Tender)was scaled as Any (tenderness present); Significant (present and interfered with limb movement). Swelling and redness were scaled as Any (swelling or redness present); Mild (0.5 centimeters \[cm\] to 2.0 cm); Moderate (2.5 to 7.0 cm); Severe (Sev) (\>7.0 cm). Participants may be represented in more than 1 category.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: The safety population included all participants who received at least 1 dose of vaccine, (n) = number of participants reporting yes for at least 1 day or no for all days.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: During the 4-day period after each dose

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Reporting Pre-Specified Systemic Events
outcomeMeasures.description: Systemic events (fever \[fv\] ≥ 37.5 degrees Celsius \[C\], fever ≥ 38 C but ≤ 39 C, fever \>39 C but ≤ 40 C, fever \> 40 C, decreased \[decr\] appetite, irritability, increased \[incr\] sleep, decreased sleep, hives, use of medication \[med\] to treat symptoms \[sx\], and use of medication to prevent symptoms) were reported using an electronic diary. Participants may be represented in more than 1 category.

outcomeMeasures.title: Geometric Mean Concentration (GMC) for Serotype-specific Pneumococcal Immunoglobulin G (IgG) Antibody 1 Month After the 3-Dose Infant Series of 13vPnC
outcomeMeasures.description: Antibody GMC for 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (Serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented. GMC (13vPnC) and corresponding 2-sided 95% confidence intervals (CI) were evaluated.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: The evaluable immunogenicity (per protocol) population was the primary analysis population consisting of eligible subjects who adhered to the protocol requirements, had valid and determinate assay results, and had no other major protocol violations; (n) = number of participants with a determinate antibody concentration for the specified serotype.

eventGroups.title: 13vPnC Infant Series
eventGroups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series). Adverse events were collected from dose 1 to approximately one month after dose 3.

eventGroups.title: 7vPnC Infant Series
eventGroups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series). Adverse events were collected from dose 1 to approximately one month after dose 3.

eventGroups.title: 13vPnC Post-Infant Series
eventGroups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series). Adverse events were collected from approximately one month after dose 3 to toddler dose.

eventGroups.title: 7vPnC Post-Infant Series
eventGroups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series). Adverse events were collected from approximately one month after dose 3 to toddler dose.

eventGroups.title: 13vPnC/13vPnC Toddler Dose
eventGroups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 12 months of age (toddler dose). Adverse events were collected for approximately one month after toddler dose.

eventGroups.title: 7vPnC/7vPnC Toddler Dose
eventGroups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 12 months of age (toddler dose). Adverse events were collected for approximately one month after toddler dose.

eventGroups.title: 7vPnC/ 13vPnC Toddler Dose
eventGroups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with a vaccine containing Pentavac at 12 months of age (toddler dose). Adverse events were collected for approximately one month after toddler dose.

eventGroups.title: 13vPnC / 13vPnC 6-Month Follow-up
eventGroups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler). Adverse events were collected for approximately six months after last visit.

eventGroups.title: 7vPnC / 7vPnC 6-Month Follow-up
eventGroups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and 12 months of age (toddler). Adverse events were collected for approximately six months after last visit.

eventGroups.title: 7vPnC / 13vPnC 6-Month Follow-up
eventGroups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with Pentavac at 2, 3, and 4 months of age (infant series) and at 12 months of age (toddler). Adverse events were collected for approximately six months after last visit.

certainAgreement.otherDetails: The PIs agreed to allow the sponsor 60 days to review and require changes to presentations or publications but only to protect confidential information and intellectual property, and for the sponsor to file a patent application, as applicable. The PIs also agreed for data to be presented first as a joint, multi-center publication.
